name: Linear Equations and Inequalities
whatIsIt: A LINEAR EQUATION like 2x + 3 = 11 has a letter to the first power and an equals sign. To SOLVE, do the SAME thing to BOTH sides to get x alone: subtract 3 (→ 2x = 8), then divide by 2 (→ x = 4). An INEQUALITY uses <, >, ≤ or ≥ and has a RANGE of answers (x + 2 > 5 means x > 3). Solve it the same way — but flip the sign if you multiply or divide by a negative.
whyNeeded: Equations model real unknowns — 'how many tickets can I buy with ₹500?'. Solving them is the core skill of algebra and appears in every science, finance and engineering formula.
realLifeUses: How many items you can buy on a budget (an inequality)
Finding an unknown in a formula
Age or number puzzles turned into equations
Break-even points in simple business maths

vocabulary.meaning: An equation with a letter to the first power and an = sign.
Do the SAME operation to both sides to keep the equation true.
A statement with <, >, ≤ or ≥ that has a range of solutions.

story.title: The Balanced Scale
story.text: Sir writes 2x + 3 = 11 and calls it a balance scale. 'Keep it level,' he says. Meera takes 3 from both sides: 2x = 8. Then she halves both sides: x = 4. 'Whatever I do to one side, I do to the other,' she says. Sir checks: 2×4 + 3 = 11. Balanced!
story.answerInStory: x = 4 — take 3 from both sides (2x = 8), then divide by 2.

visual.caption: The bar is 11; remove the constant 3 from both sides → 2x = 8; halve → x = 4.

teachingGallery.title: Do the same to both sides
teachingGallery.note: Undo with opposite operations to isolate the letter.

examples.caption: An inequality has a RANGE of answers.

standardMethod.summary: Isolate the letter by doing the same inverse operation to both sides; for inequalities, solve the same way but flip the sign when multiplying/dividing by a negative.
steps: Move the constant: do the opposite operation to BOTH sides (subtract/add).
Undo the coefficient: divide (or multiply) BOTH sides to get x alone.
Check by putting the answer back into the equation.
Inequality: same steps, but if you multiply or divide by a NEGATIVE, flip < to > (and vice versa).
So any number bigger than 4 works.

commonMistakes.mistake: Changing only one side of the equation, so it stops balancing.
commonMistakes.fix: Whatever you do, do it to BOTH sides — that keeps the equation true.

commonMistakes.mistake: Not flipping the inequality sign after dividing by a negative.
commonMistakes.fix: Multiplying or dividing an inequality by a NEGATIVE flips the sign: −2x < 6 → x > −3.

questions.q: To keep an equation true you must…

options.label: Do the same to both sides

questions.answer: Do the same to both sides

teachBackPrompt: Show a grown-up how an equation is like a balance scale: write 2x + 3 = 11 and explain each step (take 3 from both sides, then halve both sides) to find x = 4 — and check it balances.

revisionCard.summary: Linear equation: letter to the first power, with =. Solve by doing the SAME inverse operation to BOTH sides until the letter is alone; check by substituting. Inequalities (<, >, ≤, ≥) have a range of answers — solve the same way, but FLIP the sign if you multiply/divide by a negative.
